query:
INSERT INTO `program_lessons` (programId, day, title, concept, exercisePrompt, guidanceTemplate, `order`) VALUES
(30002, 1, 'The Voice Has a Name', 'Most people live their entire lives narrated by a voice they never chose. It showed up early — shaped by criticism, comparison, loss, or love that came with conditions. This week, we stop running from it and start listening. Not to obey it. To understand it.', 'Write about the most common thing your inner voice says to you. Not what you wish it said — what it actually says. When does it get loudest? What does it sound like? Is it yours, or does it sound like someone else?', 'Mirror Conversation Starter: "I want to tell you about the voice in my head. It usually shows up when I''m about to do something that matters, and it says..." | Micro-Habit: Every time you catch yourself in negative self-talk today, place your hand on your chest for three seconds. No fixing. Just notice. That''s it.', 1),
(30002, 2, 'The Origin Story', 'Your inner critic didn''t invent itself. It was built, brick by brick, from experiences that taught you that you were too much, not enough, or somehow wrong. Today we trace the blueprint.', 'Think back to the first time you remember feeling like you weren''t good enough, or that something was wrong with you. How old were you? What happened? Write it out without editing — let the memory speak.', 'Mirror Conversation Starter: "I think my inner voice started when I was young, around the time that..." | Micro-Habit: Find one photo of yourself as a child. Look at that version of you for 60 seconds. That child didn''t deserve the story they were given.', 2),
(30002, 3, 'The Lie It Tells Most', 'Every inner critic has a signature lie — the one it returns to again and again. Today we name it directly.', 'Finish this sentence 10 different ways without stopping to think: "My inner voice always comes back to telling me that I am ___." After you''ve written all 10, circle the one that lands hardest. That''s the core lie.', 'Mirror Conversation Starter: "The lie I keep believing about myself, even when I know better, is..." | Micro-Habit: Write the core lie on a piece of paper. Fold it up. Put it in a drawer. You''re not throwing it away yet — but you''re not carrying it in your pocket today.', 3),
(30002, 4, 'When It Protects You', 'Here''s the uncomfortable truth: your inner critic thinks it''s helping. It developed to protect you from rejection, failure, and pain. Today we give it credit — and then we start to question whether the protection is still necessary.', 'How has your inner critic actually protected you? What risks did it stop you from taking that might have hurt you? Be honest — it wasn''t all bad. Then ask: what did that protection cost you?', 'Mirror Conversation Starter: "I think my inner voice was trying to protect me from... but what it actually cost me was..." | Micro-Habit: Say out loud, once, to yourself: "Thank you for trying to keep me safe. I''ve got it from here."', 4),
(30002, 5, 'The Body Knows First', 'Your inner voice doesn''t just live in your head. It lives in the tightness in your chest, the shallow breath before a hard conversation, the stomach drop when you''re about to be seen. Today we learn to read the body''s language.', 'Think of a recent moment when your inner critic fired up. Before you heard the words, where did you feel it in your body? Describe the physical sensation in as much detail as you can — location, texture, temperature, weight.', 'Mirror Conversation Starter: "When my inner voice gets loud, I feel it in my body as... and it usually means I''m afraid of..." | Micro-Habit: Set three phone alarms today labeled "Body Check." When each one goes off, pause and scan: where is there tension? Breathe into it for 10 seconds.', 5),
(30002, 6, 'The Comparison Trap', 'Nothing feeds the inner critic like comparison. Social media handed it a weapon it never had before — an infinite scroll of evidence that you''re behind, less than, or doing it wrong. Today we examine the habit.', 'Who do you compare yourself to most? Write their name or describe them. What specifically do you compare? Now ask: what would you have to believe about yourself to stop needing that comparison?', 'Mirror Conversation Starter: "I compare myself to others most when I''m feeling... and what I''m really looking for when I do that is..." | Micro-Habit: Every time you open a social media app today, pause for three seconds and ask: "Am I looking for connection or comparison right now?"', 6),
(30002, 7, 'Week 1 Integration', 'You''ve spent six days listening. That takes more courage than most people realize. Today isn''t about new content — it''s about consolidating what you''ve heard. The voice is clearer now. You know its name, its origin, its favorite lie, its body signature. You''re no longer just living inside it. You''re observing it.', 'Write a letter to your inner critic. Not to fight it — to acknowledge it. Tell it what you''ve learned about it this week. Tell it what you understand now that you didn''t before. End the letter with one honest sentence about what you''re ready to change.', 'Mirror Conversation Starter: "After this first week of listening to my inner voice, the most important thing I''ve realized is..." | Micro-Habit: Read your letter from Day 3 — the one with the core lie. Then read the letter you just wrote. Notice the distance between who wrote the first one and who wrote the second.', 7);
command:
